notes:
**New**

- **Selection finally looks the way it feels.** Selecting across bullets and
  indent levels now paints one clean, continuous bar with a single straight
  left edge — bullet markers and indentation are included in the highlight,
  gaps between lines are filled, and no stray highlight fragments appear
  outside what you selected (the Dropbox-Paper feel the PRD always intended:
  "text selection flows across blocks naturally", PRD §4.2.1). The floating
  toolbar now sits above your selection instead of on top of it. Fast even in
  huge notes: selecting all of a 10,000-block note paints instantly and
  scrolling stays smooth.

**Fixes & polish**

- **Typing into a backlink card can no longer lose keystrokes.** When another
  note saved at just the wrong moment, the references panel could reorder its
  cards mid-keystroke and your last half-second of typing silently vanished —
  the panel now waits until you finish before rearranging. (PRD Addendum 1
  §A1: backlink cards are editable in place.)
- **Zooming can no longer duplicate blocks.** A sync change that moved a block
  past a section boundary while you were zoomed in could emit the same block
  twice on zoom-out; the merge is now provably one-row-per-block, with a
  built-in safety net. (PRD Addendum 20 §T: zoom is a view, never a data
  change.)
- **`[[` and `#` no longer pop suggestions inside code.** Code blocks and
  inline code keep your literal text.

**Under the hood**

- The selection overlay tracks layout reflows, renders honest per-run
  highlights on mixed-direction (RTL) lines, and virtualizes its painting for
  large documents (viewport-sliced, zero recomputation while scrolling).
- Conformance program totals after this release: 1,406 catalog scenarios,
  35 real bugs found and fixed since the catalog's creation.
